item.title: Trump buys back more US offshore wind leases in continued war on sector
item.description: US President Donald Trump scuttles four more prospective offshore wind farms as continues working to redirect renewable energy investment into oil and gas

item.title: How Equinor has quietly raised its bar for offshore wind investment
item.description: Equinor appears to have tightened the test its offshore wind projects must pass before it is willing to commit capital

item.title: Siemens Energy wins massive deal as offshore converter rig production returns to Germany
item.description: German energy tech giant to build platform at Neptun wharf in Baltic Sea port together with Smulders as country moves to larger rigs in 2GW-plus category

item.title: Orsted names new Germany head as Kubitza leaves company
item.description: Josche Muth to take over as new country manager in July after leading the utility's regulatory and public affairs for Central Europe

item.title: The UK is abuzz over floating wind alternatives, but there is a snag
item.description: There is strong interest in the new deep-water foundations, but installing turbines beyond 80-metres water depth is the pinch point

item.title: Trump 'waves white flag' over wind power freeze
item.description: Dismissal of appeal means ruling declaring 'day one' measure unlawful stands, says coalition of attorney generals

item.title: Equinor CEO admits defeat as company drops offshore wind targets
Dutch government ups offshore wind support to ensure auction demand
item.description: Minority Dutch government continueus to support new offshore wind farms after previous administration cut auction round

item.title: Europe's battery boom hits wall as lenders balk at uncertain revenues
item.description: BayWa r.e. warns that revenue uncertainty, permitting barriers and regulatory instability are preventing storage assets from being bankable

item.title: King Charles honours ex-WindEurope chief Giles Dickson
item.description: Former long-serving industry body chief recognised in King’s Birthday Honour’s

item.title: UK taps Japan for $12bn floating wind project warchest
item.description: Government says Japanese investments under Offshore Wind Compact will include developments off Scotland and Wales

item.title: Energy security worries seen boosting offshore wind long-term
item.description: Delivery challenges are still likely to push down expansion of wind at sea in the medium term, TGS | 4C quarterly reports finds

item.title: Analysts slash this year’s offshore wind investment forecast
item.description: Delivery challenges are still likely to push down expansion of wind at sea in the medium term, TGS | 4C quarterly reports finds

item.title: A radical new plan could finally solve 'wind theft' disputes
item.description: French proposal would see government step in to pay when a new offshore wind farm reduces the power output at a neighbour, and could prove a template for other European countries to follow

item.title: New York targets low-risk renewables after offshore wind turmoil
item.description: Nyserda CEO Doreen Harris told Recharge the state is looking for construction and tax credit-ready onshore projects in its latest procurement, but will it solve reliability concerns?

item.title: Siemens Gamesa chief warns over 'existential threat' to industry
item.description: Vinod Philip says faltering offshore wind expansion plans would mean downsized factories as German developers get cold feet

item.title: Deals of the Week: A $1bn renewables deal; Nordex and Mingyang orders
item.description: Middle Eastern and Chinese companies make further inroads into Europe wind power sector, while Nordex and Vestas secure wind turbine orders

item.title: Korea 'collision' could stretch Siemens Gamesa, analyst warns
item.description: Bumper response to Korean wind tender raises questions about how Siemens Gamesa could meet rivalling demands

item.title: TotalEnergies: Talks to return massive German offshore wind site have failed so far
item.description: French oil and gas major in 2024 pledged to pay German state $2.3bn for right to build and operate North Sea offshore wind farm

item.title: As a CEO, my case for wind and solar has never been stronger than in 2026
item.description: Jobs, economic benefits and the Strait of Hormuz crisis are converging to put maximum impetus behind clean power, writes Eduardo Medina of global renewable energy group RES

item.title: How the EU's growing cyber-anxiety could change wind and solar procurement
item.description: As cyber concerns are driven centre stage, future selection of technology for EU-based renewable energy projects will need to take account of more than just cost or technical suitability, said a security expert

item.title: EU grids package misses key obstacle to renewable connections, industry warns
item.description: Executives say poor grid visibility and outdated rules are preventing investment needed to connect stranded renewable capacity

item.title: Why the UK power sector needs a digital orchestrator – and what that means
item.description: While the old power system relied on telephones and control rooms for coordination, a system reliant on variable wind and solar power will need a digital orchestrator to keep the lights on, writes Angela Bishop, UK CEO of consultancy Zühlke

item.title: Siemens Gamesa, Vestas and others carry $45bn risk from past German offshore wind tenders
item.description: Up to 16GW won by five different developers in 2023-25 auctions may not reach FID stage, Aegir Insights warns

item.title: Equinor and EDF floating wind plans in doubt after Norwegian subsidy vote
item.description: Majority in Norwegian parliament reportedly demands review of planned $3.7bn in support for half gigawatt Utsira Nord site

item.title: The floating wind revolution is delayed: here's why
item.description: Floating offshore wind sector commissioned nothing and saw zero awarded through auctions in 2025 as industry body admits commercialisation will take longer than hoped

item.title: Global offshore wind outlook cut as governments told to fix 'solvable challenges'
item.description: Global Wind Energy Council sets out action plan to treat offshore wind turbines as strategic asset and ease 25GW logjam awaiting final investment decision

item.title: How a Super El Niño could impact wind and solar output in Europe
item.description: Europe is far from epicentre of El Niño events but weather phenomenon could still have marked impact on renewable energy generation – for better or worse

item.title: US wind and solar wins tax credit reprieve weeks before deadline
item.description: A Washington, DC federal court ruled that wind and solar projects have same access to critical investment and production tax credits as other clean energy sources

item.title: New Ramboll CEO ousts Orsted wind veteran and finance chief in reshuffle
item.description: New chief executive Christian Jensby says reshuffle will increase ‘agility’ of Danish consultancy that is a leading player in wind power industry
